quote: rusuy scopes over the resulting aspectual complex … V rusuy ruwe ne … nankor … kusu ne
issue: Ainu words in this paragraph are not italicized, whereas elsewhere in the chapter they are consistently given with <i lang="ain-Latn"> tags.
suggestion: Add italic markup to all Ainu forms in this paragraph: rusuy, nankor, kusu ne, etc.

issue: These Ainu phrases are not italicized, inconsistent with the book's treatment of Ainu forms in prose.
suggestion: Apply tags: mokor rusuy, cis rusuy.

quote: the distinction rests on Nakagawa's descriptive note ‹corpus-suggested›
issue: The epistemic tag ‹corpus-suggested› is used for a claim that explicitly cites a published descriptive grammar (Nakagawa 2024). The grade suggests tentative corpus evidence, but the source is an authoritative grammar; the tag should be ‹corpus-confirmed› or the description adjusted.
suggestion: Either change the tag to ‹corpus-confirmed› or revise the prose to clarify why the evidence is considered merely suggestive.

issue: This epistemic tag reads as a production metadata annotation (listing a proposer and a Discord source) rather than a standard in-text grading. It breaks the reader's flow and belongs in a footnote or endnote.
suggestion: Move the attribution to a prose footnote or a parenthetical citation: '… a lead consistent with the typological profile of head-marking, nominalization-dependent languages (proposed by nukopoli, aynu-corpora Discord 2024-11-25).'
